---
title: Edge
description: Use @justwant/env in Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge Functions, and other edge runtimes.
icon: Globe
---

## Overview

`@justwant/env/edge` is a lighter version of `@justwant/env` designed for edge runtimes where `node:fs` and file watching are unavailable. It provides the same `createEnv` and `defineEnv` API but reads environment variables from `process.env` or an inline object only — no `.env` file loading.

The `"."` export automatically resolves to the edge build when the bundler reports an `edge-light`, `workerd`, `edge`, or `worker` condition. You can also import the edge build explicitly:

```ts
import { createEnv } from "@justwant/env/edge";
```

## Cloudflare Workers

In Cloudflare Workers, `process.env` may not be available. Pass the Worker's `env` binding as `processEnv`:

```ts title="src/env.ts"
import { createEnv } from "@justwant/env/edge";
import { z } from "zod";

export function getEnv(workerEnv: Record<string, string>) {
  return createEnv({
    vars: {
      DATABASE_URL: z.string().url(),
      API_KEY: z.string().min(16),
    },
    sources: {
      processEnv: workerEnv,
    },
  });
}
```

```ts title="src/index.ts"
import { getEnv } from "./env";

export default {
  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env) {
    const config = getEnv(env as unknown as Record<string, string>);
    // config.DATABASE_URL — typed, validated
  },
};
```

## Vercel Edge Functions

```ts title="lib/env.ts"
import { createEnv } from "@justwant/env/edge";
import { z } from "zod";

export const env = createEnv({
  vars: {
    KV_URL: z.string().url(),
    NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL: z.string().url(),
  },
  clientPrefix: "NEXT_PUBLIC_",
});
```

Because Next.js replaces `process.env` at build time for edge routes, this works without additional configuration.

## Differences from the Node/Bun build

| Feature | Node/Bun | Edge |
|---------|----------|------|
| `.env` file loading | Yes | No |
| `watch` / `onReload` | Yes | No |
| `process.env` | Yes | Yes (when available) |
| Inline `sources.processEnv` object | Yes | Yes |
| Variable expansion | Yes | Yes |
| Groups | Yes | Yes |
| Validation | Yes | Yes |

**Note**: `watch: true` is silently ignored in the edge build — no error is thrown.
